final_answer:
No, there is one remaining gap; I did not change code.

What is clean:
- The old staking-status query/API is deleted.
- Validator signer keys are now checked as real secp256k1 keys.
- Genesis duplicate validator IDs are rejected.

What is still not clean:
- The bridge still has an Ethereum staking listener/processor path that ignores staking events as a no-op. That is still disabled compatibility code, not deleted code.

Why it matters: native Gold Chain validator lifecycle should not keep an Ethereum staking-event bridge path alive, even as a no-op, because it preserves the old architecture surface.

Next: delete the no-op Ethereum staking listener/processor path and its service registration/tests, leaving bridge handling only for the flows that still belong on the bridge.
prompt: Read only task, don't change code. Go through fine tooth comb of what you just implemented and check for gaps or partial implementation and report back please. Dont be overzealous and go on tangents, remain in the confines of the scope of this task, there might be times where you might have to say all done nothing left, which would also be an acceptable response
